title: The Shadow House
summary: Emily visits her friend Max at his new big house today.
coverScene: A warm light shines through a window of a large, old house with a sprawling garden, where two friends, Emily and Max, sit on a bench together.

sentences.text: Emily traveled to the country to visit her friend Max at his new home.
The house was big and old, standing alone on a high hill.
It was surrounded by dark trees that moved slowly in the cold wind.
Emily had known Max since they were children, and she had always liked this strong friendship.
She felt happy to see him when he opened the heavy front door with a wide smile.
Max helped her with her bags and led her inside the warm living room.
A bright fire was burning in the room, which made the place feel comfortable.
However, Emily could still feel a cold draft coming from the corners of the room.
Max explained that he had recently bought the property and was still learning the history of the place.
He was very excited to show her around his new place.
He believed that this house, which was built in the last century, was perfect for him.
The next morning, Emily woke up early and decided to explore the building.
She wanted to find the kitchen to make some hot coffee before Max got up.
She opened her bedroom door and stepped into the long, quiet hall.
She walked down the path, expecting to find the stairs at the end.
Instead, she found herself in a different section of the house.
The doors in this hall led to different rooms that she had not seen during her first day.
She opened a white door, hoping to find the stairs, but it only led to a small, empty bedroom.
She turned back to return to her room, but the hall now looked completely different.
The doors that she had just passed seemed to have changed position.
She realized that she was lost in the house, and a sudden wave of fear came over her.
If she had brought her mobile phone, she would have called Max for help.
She began to call his name with a loud voice, hoping that he would hear her.
After some time, she heard Max calling her name from a distance.
He found her standing in a narrow hall, looking pale and scared.
He laughed gently and said that it was easy to get lost in such a large place.
Emily tried to explain that the doors had actually changed, but Max did not believe her.
He thought she was simply confused because she was tired from the journey.
To make her feel better, they decided to look for some information about the building.
Emily and Max tried to find a map of the house that could help them understand the structure.
If they had a map, they would easily find the way around the many halls.
They went to the library, which was a large room filled with old books and papers.
They searched through boxes on a big desk and looked behind the pictures on the walls.
But they could not find one.
They only found some old letters and a small key that did not seem to fit any lock.
During the day, they spent time talking and working in the garden.
Max was very happy, but Emily could not stop thinking about her experience in the hall.
She kept watching the doors, wondering if they were moving when she was not looking.
That night, they heard strange noises that seemed to come from the walls and the floors.
The sounds were louder and more frequent than they had been on the previous night.
A heavy sound was heard from the floor above her room.
Emily was scared, but Max was calm as he listened to the sounds.
He said it was just the house settling, which was normal for such an old structure.
He told her not to worry and to try to get some sleep.
But Emily knew what she saw, and she was sure that the house was changing every day.
If she had stayed at her own home, she would have slept well without any fear.
The next morning, Emily decided to prove her theory to Max.
She led him to the living room and pointed to a window.
The window, which had shown the beautiful garden yesterday, now looked out onto a solid stone wall.
Max stared at the stone wall in silence, his face turning pale with surprise.
He finally realized that Emily was telling the truth about the changes.
A strange sound was heard from the hall, and they both turned to look.
They decided to explore the house together to find the reason for the changes.
They wanted to know what was causing the rooms to shift.
Max took a light, and Emily carried the small key they had found in the library.
They stepped into the hall, ready to face whatever mystery lay ahead of them.
As they walked, the rooms shifted around them in a silent and strange dance.
It was like the house was playing a trick on them, trying to keep them lost.
They would walk through a door and find themselves in a place they had never seen before.
The air became colder as they went deeper into the center of the building.
If they did not stay close together, they would easily be separated by the moving walls.
Suddenly, they saw a small wooden door at the end of a narrow passage.
The door was different from all the others, and it had a small hole for the key.
Emily realized that this was the door that the small key would open.
She gave the key to Max, who put it into the hole and turned it slowly.
The door opened with a soft sound, revealing a hidden room in the heart of the house.
Inside the room, they found a beautiful model of the entire house.
The model was made of wood and had tiny rooms that were moving by themselves.
They realized that the house was built by a man who loved complex games.
The movements of the house were controlled by a system of gears beneath the floor.
Max found a small switch on the side of the model and turned it off.
Immediately, the strange noises stopped, and the house became completely still.
They walked back to the living room and saw that the window now showed the garden again.
They both felt a great sense of relief as they looked out at the green trees.
Max thanked Emily for her help and her strong support during the adventure.
If she had not insisted on exploring, they might never have solved the mystery.
They were happy to know that the house was finally safe and stable.

quiz.q: What did Emily do at Max's new home?
options: She got lost in the house
She found a map of the house
She heard strange noises
The house was big and old
The house was changing every day
Max was acting strange

quiz.q: What did Emily and Max decide to do?
options: They decided to leave the house
They decided to explore the house
They decided to call for help

sentences.text: Emily decided to visit her old friend, Max.
He has recently bought a new home in the country.
She has never seen such a large building before.
The journey was long, but she was excited to see his new place.
When she arrived, she stood in front of the building.
The house was very big and looked extremely old.
It had dark wood and large windows that seemed to watch her.
Max opened the door with a wide smile on his face.
He was very happy to welcome his friend.
Emily felt really glad to see him after such a long time.
Max immediately showed Emily around the large house.
They walked through many different areas together.
He showed her the kitchen, the living room, and her bedroom.
She loved the high walls and the old style of the building.
Seeing her friend make a new start in this place made her feel warm inside.
The next day, Emily woke up early.
She wanted to find the kitchen to make some hot tea.
However, she quickly got lost in the huge house.
She opened a wood door, expecting to find the steps.
Instead, the door led to a completely different room.
It was a dark library that she had not seen before.
She tried another door, but it led to a small room.
None of the doors seemed to go where they should.
Emily finally found Max in the main hall.
She told him about her strange experience.
They decided that they needed a map of the house.
Emily and Max tried to find a map in every room.
They searched inside old desks and looked through many books.
They checked the boxes in the kitchen and looked in the library.
But they could not find a map anywhere.
It was as if no map of this house has ever existed.
When night came, the house became very quiet.
But then, they started to hear strange noises.
It sounded like the walls were moving.
Emily did not sleep in her bed, listening closely.
She heard soft steps and deep sounds behind the walls.
It felt like the whole structure was changing around them.
The sounds continued for hours until the morning light appeared.
At breakfast, Emily was very scared.
She told Max about the terrible noises.
However, Max remained completely calm.
He smiled and told her not to worry.
He said that old houses always make strange noises.
"It is just the wood of the house settling," Max explained.
He believed there was a simple, natural reason for everything.
But his words did not make Emily feel any better.
But Emily knew what she saw with her own eyes.
She knew that the house was different every day.
Yesterday, the blue room was next to her bedroom.
Today, that same door led to a long, dark hall.
The windows were in different positions too.
If she walked down the steps, she arrived in a new place.
The changes were real, and she could not ignore them.
Finally, Emily convinced Max that something was wrong.
They decided to explore the house together.
They wanted to find the reason for these strange changes.
"If we do not find the reason, we will get lost here," Emily said.
They took a piece of paper to write down what they saw.
They started at the front door and began their walk.
They were ready to solve the problem of this changing house.
As they walked, the rooms shifted around them.
They turned a corner and found themselves in the garden.
But they had not opened any outside doors.
They turned back, but the hall had disappeared.
Now, there was only a strong wall behind them.
It was like the house was playing a trick on them.
The house was alive, and it did not want them to leave.

quiz.q: Why did Emily visit the house?
options: To visit her old friend Max at his new home.
To help Max paint the walls.
To buy the house from Max.

quiz.q: What did Max believe about the strange noises at night?
options: He thought someone was breaking in.
He believed it was just the wood of the house settling.
He thought the house was alive.

quiz.q: What happened when Emily and Max tried to explore the house together?
options: They immediately found a map on the wall.
The rooms shifted and a hall disappeared behind them.
They decided to leave the house and go to a hotel.

sentences.text: Emily visited her friend Max at his new home.
The house was very big.
It was also very old.
The house had many dark windows.
Emily looked at the large building.
She went to the front door.
Max opened the door with a smile.
He welcomed his friend inside.
Max showed Emily around the old house.
They walked through many cold rooms.
Emily felt very happy to see her friend.
She had not seen Max for a long time.
They talked about his new life here.
Max loved the big, quiet place.
But Emily felt a little cold inside the house.
The walls were too high and dark.
The next day, Emily got lost in the house.
She wanted to find her friend Max.
But she could not find the right way.
She opened a small blue door.
It did not lead to his room.
Instead, it led to a dark, cold room.
She opened another door.
This door led to a long, low room.
The doors led to completely different rooms now.
Emily began to feel fear.
Emily found Max and told him about the doors.
They decided to look for a plan of the house.
"A plan will help," Max said.
They looked in the room with many books.
They looked inside old boxes and under tables.
They looked through many old papers.
But they could not find a plan in any place.
"We do not have a plan," Emily said.
"We are going to get lost again."
At night, they went to sleep.
But they heard different sounds in the dark.
The sounds came from the walls.
They heard a low sound like a step.
It sounded like someone was walking.
But there was no one there.
The house seemed to be changing.
The walls seemed to move.
It was a very long night for them.
They could not sleep well.
In the morning, Emily felt a lot of fear.
But Max did not feel fear.
"Do not worry," Max said.
"Old houses always make these sounds."
"The walls are just moving a little."
He smiled at her.
He did not think there was a problem.
"We are okay here," he said.
But Emily did not believe him.
But Emily knew what she saw.
She saw the rooms change.
The house was different every day.
Yesterday, the room was short.
Today, the same room was very long.
She saw a blue door yesterday.
Today, that door was not there.
Something was very wrong with this house.
She could feel it in her heart.
"We must look at the house together," she said.
Max agreed to go with her.
They wanted to find the reason for the changes.
They started to walk through the house.
They held hands because they were cold.
They walked into the dark.
They wanted to see everything.
As they walked, the rooms changed around them.
The walls moved around them.
The doors opened and closed by themselves.
They could not go back to the start.
It was like the house was playing a game.
It wanted to keep them inside.
Max finally felt fear too.
"Where are we?" Max asked.
But the house did not answer.

quiz.q: Why did Emily get lost in the house?
options: Because the doors led to different rooms.
Because she did not have a phone.
Because Max did not want to see her.

quiz.q: What did Emily and Max look for in the house?
options: Some new books.
A plan of the house.
A different door.

quiz.q: What did Max think about the sounds at first?
options: He thought they were a big problem.
He thought they were from a game.
He thought they were normal for old houses.
